title: SaplingIvkDecryptor holds IVK scalar with no Zeroize on drop
description: wallet/src/scanner.rs SaplingIvkDecryptor holds ivk: SaplingIvk (a jubjub::Fr scalar) as a struct field with no Zeroize or custom Drop implementation. The IVK scalar stays in process memory until the allocator reuses the heap page. An attacker with memory-read access (core dump, swap file, /proc/pid/mem) can recover the IVK and use it to trial-decrypt any on-chain Sapling note to determine if it belongs to this wallet — nie-kef6.55 and nie-kef6.77 track zeroization for WASM/FFI byte buffers but the wallet-crate Rust type itself is not covered.
